title: Svaeng-Yul
tagline: Khmer-narrated video courses for Cambodian tech students
coverImageAlt: Svaeng Yul admin tracks screen showing AI Engineering, Data Science, Cybersecurity, and Software Engineering tracks
problem: Cambodian students who want to learn AI engineering, data science, cybersecurity, and software engineering need a self-study path that explains technical topics in Khmer instead of leaving them to translate everything alone.
context: Svaeng Yul pivoted from a medical QCM practice app into a calm video learning platform for Cambodian tech students. The structure is Tracks, Courses, and Lessons, with Khmer-narrated video, an English UI, student progress, an admin CMS, and an AI lesson assistant grounded in the current lesson transcript.
constraints: Must run in demo mode with no Supabase or video-provider credentials
Live mode needs Supabase Auth/Postgres and Bunny Stream without leaking server keys
Draft tracks, courses, and lessons must never appear on public routes
AI support must stay scoped to the lesson instead of becoming a generic chatbot
myRole: I designed and built the pivot: the data model, demo/live repository layer, learning routes, progress tracking, admin CMS, Bunny Stream integration path, and AI lesson assistant.
solution: A dual-mode architecture: one repository interface with an in-memory demo implementation and a Supabase implementation for live use. Demo mode seeds tracks, courses, lessons, progress, and demo users. Live mode adds Supabase Auth/Postgres, RLS, Bunny Stream upload/playback, and server-side admin protection.
callout: The product direction is Khmer-first learning content, not just quiz practice: video lessons, track structure, progress, admin publishing, and lesson-grounded AI help.
tradeoffs: Reused the strong Next/Supabase foundation from the QCM app instead of doing a clean-slate rewrite.
Kept UI copy English while Khmer appears in lesson narration, matching the current content strategy.
Made demo mode first-class so the app remains reviewable without paid video or database credentials.
whatIBuilt: Track, course, and lesson model for AI Engineering, Data Science, Cybersecurity, and Software Engineering.
Student home with continue-watching and per-track progress.
Course detail pages and lesson player with curriculum sidebar, progress actions, and previous/next navigation.
Admin CMS for tracks, courses, lessons, publishing, video upload flow, and student list.
AI lesson assistant scoped to the current lesson transcript, with a daily rate limit.
Supabase schema, RLS policies, and seed content for live mode.
whatILearned: How to pivot an existing domain without throwing away reusable architecture.
How to design one data-layer interface that serves both a credential-free demo and a real Postgres backend.
How to keep draft learning content out of public routes even when using service-role reads internally.
How to integrate video hosting without shipping provider secrets to the client.
How to scope AI help to a lesson transcript so it supports study instead of replacing it.
results: Runs in demo mode with in-memory seeded tracks, courses, lessons, and demo accounts.
Live mode path supports Supabase Auth/Postgres and Bunny Stream video hosting.
Includes unit tests, Playwright smoke coverage, lint, strict TypeScript, and production build scripts.

screenshots.caption: Admin CMS track list after the pivot: AI Engineering, Data Science, Cybersecurity, and Software Engineering.

repoNote: Source repository is private because Svaeng Yul is an active startup asset. Public proof is provided through the demo, current screenshot, case study, and validation notes. The product has pivoted from an earlier medical QCM practice app into the Khmer-narrated tech video learning platform described here.
